name: Mineral salts medium (MSM), 4-fold buffered, with 1,6-hexamethylenediamine (HMDA) as sole carbon source (PMC11879879)
description: Mineral salts medium (MSM), 4-fold buffered, with 1,6-hexamethylenediamine (HMDA) as sole carbon source — medium reported in PMC11879879, extracted for GrowthDB and mapped to BiGG.

provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC11879879 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Composition mapped to BiGG (mapper + salt dissociation); presence-based bounds; paper_amount retained. Added via GrowthDB curation.
provenance.formulation_warning: workflow correction sparse; kept original — The paper defines this medium as the standard Wierckx/Hartmans P. putida mineral salts medium (MSM), cited to Wierckx et al. 2005 Appl. Environ. Microbiol. 71:8221 (ref. 57), with the buffer strength scaled 4-fold. Only the phosphate buffer (15.52 g/L K2HPO4 + 6.52 g/L NaH2PO4; these are 4x the standard 1x values 3.88 and 1.63 g/L) and the carbon source (15 mM HMDA) are stated verbatim in THIS paper. The base salts ((NH4)2SO4 N-source, MgCl2, trace element solution) come from the cited standard MSM recipe, so their exact amounts are not given in this paper - amounts marked accordingly. The prior auto-extracted formulation was incomplete: it OMITTED the defining carbon source (HMDA, 1,6-hexamethylenediamine), the nitrogen source, magnesium and all trace elements, and only listed raw salt-dissociation ions (K, Na, phosphate, O2). Organism P. putida KT2440 is an obligate aerobe grown in shaken cultures (180-225 rpm), so oxygen corrected from 'facultative' to 'aerobic'. HMDA also serves as sole N source in the C/N-source cultivations.
provenance.verification_evidence: P. putida KT2440 strains were cultivated in 4-fold buffered (15.52 g l-1 K2HPO4 and 6.52 g l-1 NaH2PO4) MSM medium ... MSM containing 15 mM of HMDA, Ahx or e-caprolactam as sole carbon source.

oxygen_note: no oxygen requirement specified; O2 available but not asserted
